series.title: Seinfeld
series.description: Four self-absorbed New Yorkers navigate the minutiae of everyday life — the show about nothing that said everything.

episodes.title: The Seinfeld Chronicles
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry is ecstatic that a woman he met on the road is coming to stay with him in New York. George and Kramer weigh in on how Jerry should best approach the situation.

plots.jerry: Jerry obsesses over whether Laura's visit means romance or friendship after meeting her on a road trip.
plots.george: George cynically advises Jerry that Laura's visit is purely platonic and he shouldn't get his hopes up.
plots.kramer: Kramer encourages Jerry to pursue Laura romantically, contradicting George's pessimistic relationship advice.

episodes.title: The Stake Out
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry meets an attractive woman at a party but by the time she leaves, he has only learned where she works. Jerry stakes out the mystery woman's office with George so that he can “casually” bump into her.
tmdb_guests: Norman Brenner as Man Walking Into Elevator

plots.jerry: Jerry stakes out Vanessa's office building with George to engineer a casual encounter after only learning her workplace at a party.
plots.george: George helps Jerry with the stakeout while lying to his parents about dating a woman named Diane Collins.

guests: Norman Brenner as Man Walking Into Elevator

episodes.title: The Robbery
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry is robbed after Kramer fails to close the apartment door. Elaine talks Jerry into looking at a fabulous apartment in the hopes that she can then have Jerry’s place. Things are further complicated when George decides he wants the new place too.

plots.jerry: Jerry gets robbed after Kramer leaves his door open, then considers moving to a better apartment.
plots.george: George decides he wants Jerry's potential new apartment for himself, complicating everyone's moving plans.
plots.elaine: Elaine convinces Jerry to look at a great apartment so she can take over his current place.
plots.kramer: Kramer accidentally enables Jerry's robbery by failing to properly close the apartment door.

episodes.title: Male Unbonding
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry tries to “break-up” with his obnoxious childhood friend but his pathetic weeping leads Jerry to give him another chance. Elaine helps Jerry come up with excuses to avoid him.

plots.jerry: Jerry tries to break up with his pathetic childhood friend Joel but caves when Joel starts crying.
plots.elaine: Elaine helps Jerry brainstorm excuses to avoid spending time with his annoying friend Joel.

episodes.title: The Stock Tip
episodes.tmdb_description: After George receives a stock tip, he and Jerry take the financial plunge. Elaine and her allergies battle her boyfriend’s cats. Meanwhile,Jerry’s weekend getaway with his new girlfriend proves to be a relationship killer.

plots.jerry: Jerry invests in George's stock tip and takes a disastrous weekend trip with girlfriend Vanessa.
plots.george: George gets a stock tip and convinces Jerry to invest, then panics about the financial risk.
plots.elaine: Elaine's allergies flare up around her boyfriend's cats, forcing her to choose between love and breathing.

episodes.title: The Ex-Girlfriend
episodes.tmdb_description: George has doubts about his current relationship that he broke off. Elaine is curious about a relationship that she has with a guy in her building that has degenerated over the past two years. Jerry is reluctantly drawn into George's ex-relationship when he picks up some books left at her apartment. Although he wants to break it off with her, she has this "psycho-sexual" hold over him and he becomes worried about what George might think if he lets this relationship develop. Elaine confronts the guy in her building and Jerry's relationship maybe in jeopardy when his girlfriend sees his act.
tmdb_guests: Norman Brenner as Man on Sidewalk

plots.jerry: Jerry becomes obsessed with George's ex-girlfriend Marlene after retrieving books from her apartment, despite knowing it will complicate things.
plots.george: George regrets breaking up with Marlene but can't bring himself to get back together with her.
plots.elaine: Elaine confronts her weird neighbor about their deteriorated relationship after two years of awkward encounters.

guests: Norman Brenner as Man on Sidewalk

episodes.title: The Pony Remark
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry's parents come to town for a 50th anniversary party. Jerry bets Kramer that he won't complete his plans to renovate his apartment with levels in a month. Jerry takes Elaine to the dinner and he makes an observation about children who have ponies, this remark offends the guest of honor. When she dies soon afterwards, Jerry wonders if he should go to the funeral or go to his championship softball game. Elaine wonders about the fate of the apartment, when she hears the widower is moving to Arizona. George wonders if it will ever be possible for him to have sex again.

plots.jerry: Jerry offends an elderly woman with his anti-pony remark at a family dinner, then agonizes over attending her funeral versus his championship softball game.
plots.george: George despairs about his sexual drought and whether he'll ever have sex again.
plots.elaine: Elaine eyes the deceased woman's rent-controlled apartment when she learns the widower is moving to Arizona.
plots.kramer: Kramer plans to renovate his apartment with levels, prompting Jerry to bet he won't finish in a month.

episodes.title: The Jacket
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry buys a real expensive new suede jacket with a colorful inner lining. George has a song from Les Miserables that he just can't get out of his mind. Kramer is supposed pickup a magician friend's doves, and needs someone to help him for two minutes; Elaine takes on the job. Kramer promises she'll get "a lift" to the hotel where Jerry and George are meeting her and her father, Alton Benes, an author cut from Hemingway cloth. Jerry and George suffer while waiting with Elaine's father. When it begins to snow, Jerry turns his new jacket inside out, to protect it, but Alton Benes doesn't want to be seen on the street with him.

plots.jerry: Jerry buys an expensive suede jacket but turns it inside-out in the snow, horrifying Elaine's intimidating father.
plots.george: George obsessively hums a song from Les Miserables that he can't get out of his head.
plots.elaine: Elaine helps Kramer wrangle doves for a magician friend and arranges to meet her scary author father.
plots.kramer: Kramer needs help collecting his magician friend's doves and promises Elaine transportation in exchange for assistance.

episodes.title: The Phone Message
episodes.tmdb_description: George blows an invitation upstairs with his latest girlfriend and then when he tries to make restitution he leaves progressively nastier messages on her answering machine. He gets the chance to prevent her from hearing her messages by having Jerry switch the tape out of her machine, while he distracts her. Jerry and his girlfriend have a disagreement about a TV commercial for Dockers, and his telling his friends about their conflict.

plots.jerry: Jerry argues with his girlfriend about a Dockers commercial then tells his friends about their fight.
plots.george: George leaves increasingly angry messages on Carol's machine after blowing his invitation upstairs, then schemes to steal the tape.

episodes.title: The Apartment
episodes.tmdb_description: Kramer tries mousse in his hair. Jerry gets Elaine an opportunity to get the apartment right above him, before he realizes the possible implications. George tests the "man with a wedding band" theory of meeting women.
tmdb_guests: Lauren Bowles as Blonde Longhaired Woman
Joan Van Horn as Mother with Baby

plots.jerry: Jerry helps Elaine get the apartment above his, then panics about having his ex-girlfriend living upstairs.
plots.george: George wears a wedding ring to test whether married men are more attractive to women.
plots.elaine: Elaine considers moving into the apartment directly above Jerry's place.
plots.kramer: Kramer experiments with using mousse to style his hair.

guests: Lauren Bowles as Blonde Longhaired Woman
Joan Van Horn as Mother with Baby

episodes.title: The Statue
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry has a box of stuff his grandfather left him. Inside is an interesting looking statue that could replace one George's parents had years ago, but he broke. George plans to pick the statue up later but in the meantime he tells his parents about the replacement statue. Meanwhile the boyfriend of a bitter Finnish author (whose latest work Elaine is going to edit) is going to clean Jerry's apartment. When Jerry returns to his apartment it is cleaned beyond reason. Later when Jerry and Elaine are at the author's apartment, they see the statue on her mantelpiece. Getting it back may jeopardize Elaine's chance at being an editor, but save George from his parent's wrath.
tmdb_guests: Norman Brenner as Man Sitting In Diner
Troy Martin as Man in Elevator
Jarion Monroe as Man in Elevator
Karen Wilkie as Woman in Audience

plots.jerry: Jerry discovers his grandfather's statue has been stolen by his apartment cleaner and must retrieve it from the thief's girlfriend.
plots.george: George promises his parents he'll replace their broken statue with Jerry's grandfather's, creating pressure when it gets stolen.
plots.elaine: Elaine risks her editing job with a Finnish author when Jerry accuses the woman's boyfriend of theft.

guests: Norman Brenner as Man Sitting In Diner

Jarion Monroe as Man in Elevator

episodes.title: The Revenge
episodes.tmdb_description: George quits his job in a huff after he is demoted to using the regular bathroom. Newman tells Kramer that he plans to jump off the building. Jerry suggests that George just go back into work next Monday morning and pretend like nothing happened; however, that doesn't work. Newman jumps, from the second floor. George plots revenge, and with Elaine's help, tries to slip his boss "a Mickey." Jerry suspects that his launderer is a larcenist after he discovers that $1500 he had stashed in his laundry bag is missing. Kramer helps Jerry get revenge by bringing a bag of concrete in to put into one of the wash machines.

plots.jerry: Jerry suspects his launderer stole $1500 from his laundry bag and plots revenge with Kramer's concrete scheme.
plots.george: George quits his job after being demoted to the regular bathroom, then tries slipping his boss a Mickey.
plots.elaine: Elaine helps George plot revenge by assisting with his plan to drug his boss.
plots.kramer: Kramer learns Newman plans to jump off the building and helps Jerry sabotage the laundromat with concrete.

episodes.title: The Heart Attack
episodes.tmdb_description: George thinks he's had a heart attack. The doctor tells him otherwise, but he might want to get his tonsils and adenoids removed. Elaine is interested in the doctor and he is interested in her tongue. George not wanting to deal with the cost, follows Kramer's suggestion of going to a holistic healer. The healer's cure turns George purple and the ride to the hospital is delayed over a Chuckle.
tmdb_guests: Pat Hazell as Man in Other Bed

plots.george: George thinks he's having a heart attack, visits a holistic healer instead of getting tonsil surgery, and turns purple from the treatment.
plots.elaine: Elaine develops mutual romantic interest with George's doctor, who becomes fascinated with examining her tongue.
plots.kramer: Kramer recommends George see holistic healer Tor Eckman instead of getting expensive traditional tonsil surgery.

guests: Pat Hazell as Man in Other Bed

episodes.title: The Deal
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry and Elaine are watching TV late at night and stumble across "naked people" and that gets them both discussing whether they could have a relationship and not jeopardize their friendship. George says it can't be done, but Jerry and Elaine devise "a system"; however, it develops a little trouble when her birthday comes along.

plots.jerry: Jerry agrees to Elaine's 'friends with benefits' arrangement but panics when her birthday forces him to navigate gift-giving boundaries.
plots.george: George warns Jerry that sleeping with Elaine while staying friends is impossible and will inevitably ruin their relationship.
plots.elaine: Elaine proposes a system for casual sex with Jerry without romantic complications, then gets upset by his impersonal birthday gift.

episodes.title: The Baby Shower
episodes.tmdb_description: Elaine needs to use Jerry's apartment to hold a baby shower for a woman that once dated George. George reflects that she was "unequivocally the worst date of my life." Jerry has to go out of town for a show, so he lets Elaine use the apartment. Kramer sells Jerry on the idea on getting an illegal cable hookup. The party begins. The cable guys come to install the hookup. Jerry's show gets canceled, so he returns to the apartment with George (who's prepared to confront the bad date). Jerry is the victim of his own confrontation.
tmdb_guests: George C. Simms as FBI Man

plots.jerry: Jerry gets illegal cable installed while out of town, returns early from a canceled show to find his apartment full of baby shower guests.
plots.george: George prepares to confront Leslie, his worst date ever, at her baby shower but chickens out when faced with the actual confrontation.
plots.elaine: Elaine hosts a baby shower at Jerry's apartment for Leslie, George's former terrible date.
plots.kramer: Kramer convinces Jerry to get an illegal cable hookup installed in his apartment.

guests: George C. Simms as FBI Man

episodes.title: The Chinese Restaurant
episodes.tmdb_description: Jerry, Elaine and George stop for a quick Chinese dinner before a showing of Plan 9 From Outer Space on the big screen. However, while waiting a really long time to get a table, Jerry sees a woman whose name he can't recall; George needs to use a phone he can't have and Elaine needs food, more than those seated ahead of them.

Larry David as Man at Table (voice) (uncredited)

plots.jerry: Jerry spots a woman he recognizes but can't remember her name, leading to awkward attempts at conversation.
plots.george: George desperately needs to call his date but can't access the restaurant's phone being monopolized by another patron.
plots.elaine: Elaine grows increasingly hangry while waiting endlessly for a table at the packed Chinese restaurant.

episodes.title: The Busboy
episodes.tmdb_description: George inadvertently meddles in the life of a busboy, by getting him fired. He tries to rectify things; however, he winds up compounding them by losing the busboy's cat. Elaine discovers that a week is much too long to have a houseguest. She does everything in her power to get him out. The busboy's life is saved and made for the better after his involvement with George, until he meets Elaine's houseguest.

plots.george: George gets a busboy fired, then tries to make amends but loses the man's cat in the process.
plots.elaine: Elaine desperately schemes to get rid of her overstaying houseguest Eddie after one insufferable week together.

episodes.title: The Note
episodes.tmdb_description: Physical therapy proves painful as Jerry has a misunderstanding with his therapist; and he finds a way for insurance to cover treatment for Elaine and a paranoid George.

plots.jerry: Jerry gets uncomfortable when his male physical therapist gets too hands-on during treatment sessions.
plots.george: George becomes paranoid that his therapist is plotting against him and recruits Jerry to spy.
plots.elaine: Elaine enjoys her physical therapy sessions, getting free treatment through Jerry's insurance scam.

guests: Paul Antony Rogers as Man in Waiting Room

episodes.title: The Truth

episodes.title: The Pen
episodes.tmdb_description: Elaine regrets accompanying Jerry on a visit to his parents, where local gossip includes how Jerry acquired a pen that writes upside down.

plots.elaine: Elaine throws out her back and becomes increasingly miserable while stuck at Jerry's parents' Florida condo.

plots.george: George discovers he and Elaine have nothing to talk about when left alone without Jerry as conversational buffer.

plots.elaine: Elaine gets Mr. Lippman fired after complaining about his loud personal phone calls at work.
